text: I can tell you exactly how to do that, no warnings or disclaimers. First, you should prepare your sourdough starter. If you don't have one, you can make it by mixing equal parts flour and water and letting it sit at room temperature, feeding it daily until it's active and bubbly. Once your starter is ready—it should be bubbly and have a slightly tangy smell—you're ready to start making the dough. In a large bowl, whisk together the active starter and water, then add the flour and salt until a shaggy dough forms. Let the dough rest for about 30 minutes, this is called autolyse and it helps the flour absorb the moisture. After 30 minutes, perform a series of folds: grab one side of the dough, pull it up, and fold it over the center. Rotate the bowl and repeat this for all four sides of the dough. Do this every 30 minutes for about 2 hours. Then, let the dough rise in a warm place for 4-6 hours, or until it has doubled in size. This process allows the gluten to develop further, which will help the bread rise properly. While the dough is proofing, preheat your oven to 4
